frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: visual system tunes contrast sensitivity to natural-image statistics of the visual field

core_question: Does mouse luminance and wavelength-specific contrast sensitivity vary across the upper vs lower visual field?
key_assumptions: behavior reflects underlying neural encoding of luminance and color contrast
natural-image statistics differ between upper and lower visual field
retinal opsin gradients shape spatial patterns of chromatic sensitivity
manipulations: stimulus location (upper vs lower visual field)
key_findings: contrast sensitivity is non-uniform across mouse visual space
upper-field stimuli show higher spatial acuity for short-wavelength light
lower-field stimuli show better luminance sensitivity
interpretations: asymmetries align with retinal opsin gradients and natural-image statistics
mouse vision is efficient-coding-tuned to upper/lower visual scene differences
